message: fix(asupersync): wire missing _with_cx methods to unblock --no-default-features build (ft-6piwt)

@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4qom2","title":"[SEV] tmux cmd_queue cap only enforced at pop site; unbounded growth when state != Idle","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue has CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000 enforced only inside send_next_command at line 289 via cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286-288 when state != Idle, so the drain-oldest logic never runs if tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse.

Four push sites append to cmd_queue with NO cap check:
- line 196: Event::LayoutChange -> ListAllPanes
- line 223: Event::SessionChanged -> ListCommands
- line 234: Event::WindowAdd -> ListAllWindows
- line 375: split_pane (user split) -> SplitPane

Concrete interleaving: (1) tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse; (2) tmux server is slow/hung (real-world scenario: remote tmux on lagging SSH link, tmux server overloaded, Event::Guarded response delayed); (3) GUI user resizes window repeatedly -> stream of Event::LayoutChange -> each push_back at line 196 runs without cap check; (4) send_next_command early-returns because state != Idle. Queue grows unbounded -> memory leak, eventual OOM.

The CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH comment at line 32-33 explicitly advertises 'prevent unbounded memory growth during event storms' as a safety invariant. That invariant is currently only upheld at the pop site, where it can't run under the exact condition (stuck tmux) that event storms exploit.

Fix: extract the cap-enforcement logic into a push_command_capped helper that applies the drain-oldest + warn/error logging at every push site. The helper runs under the cmd_queue lock (just acquired by the caller), so it's atomic with the push and has no additional locking cost.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:17:26.601099Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.160715Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T00:21:03.158111Z","close_reason":"Completed: added push_command_capped() helper and routed all 4 production push sites (LayoutChange/SessionChanged/WindowAdd/split_pane) through it. The hard cap is now enforced at push time, not only at pop time — closing the unbounded-growth window when tmux is stuck in State::WaitingForResponse. cargo check passes clean. Commit 1055cffc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-682n6","title":"Migrate ~50 tokio-based async tests to asupersync runtime (ft-22x4r tracked)","description":"# Goal
Convert ~50 tests using #[tokio::test] to work with the asupersync runtime. These tests fail at runtime with: task::spawn called outside of Runtime::block_on context. Originally tracked as ft-22x4r.

# Background
The asupersync migration replaced tokio, but ~50 tests still use #[tokio::test]. Count verified 2026-04-06: 50 in .rs files (excluding .bak). Most are in *_labruntime.rs test files (2 per file across ~25 files) plus 2 in dependency_eradication.rs.

# Design
## Discovery (exact count at implementation time)
1. grep -r '#\[tokio::test\]' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v '.bak' > /tmp/tokio_tests.txt
2. Count and categorize by file pattern:
   - *_labruntime.rs files: ~25 files × 2 tests = ~50 tests (async smoke + basic scenario)
   - dependency_eradication.rs: 2 tests
3. For each, identify tokio primitives used: spawn, sleep, channels, timeout

## Migration strategy
- *_labruntime.rs pattern: These all follow the same template (2 tests per file). Once one is migrated, the rest are mechanical.
  Replace: #[tokio::test] with #[test] + runtime_compat::block_on(async { ... })
  Or: if asupersync provides a test macro, use that
- Replace tokio::spawn -> runtime_compat spawn equivalent
- Replace tokio::time::sleep -> runtime_compat::sleep
- Replace tokio channels -> runtime_compat channels

## Batch approach
Since *_labruntime.rs files follow a uniform pattern, write a migration helper that does the mechanical replacement, then verify each file compiles.

## Structured Logging
- Migration log: file, old_macro, new_macro, primitives_replaced, compile_status

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Zero #[tokio::test] in .rs files (verified by grep, excluding .bak).
2. All ~50 previously-failing tests now pass.
3. No semantic changes — same assertions.
4. Total test count preserved.
5. No new tokio dependencies.
6. Commit message lists affected files.

# Implementation Notes
- runtime_compat.rs provides bridge primitives.
- The *_labruntime.rs naming convention suggests these are lab/smoke tests for async behavior.
- This bead unblocks ft-xbnl0.2 (async cutover epic).
- Coordinate with ft-n2j5w (MCP test fix) if any MCP tests also use tokio::test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:58:21.227738Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.155157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:15.154811Z","close_reason":"Verified: zero actual #[tokio::test] annotations remain in crates/frankenterm-core/. All 50 occurrences found by grep are in comments/docstrings/string literals describing the migration that was already done. The *_labruntime.rs files already use #[test] + RuntimeFixture::current_thread().block_on(). No code changes needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","migration","testing"]}
+{"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-akx00","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","design":"# Execution Strategy
1. Start with inventory discipline so no suspect disappears mid-stream.
2. Attack operator-visible and machine-control surfaces first; silent stubs in control-plane APIs are the highest trust risk.
3. Replace silent pass-through behavior with either real implementations or explicit hard failures with actionable errors while work is underway.
4. Prefer shared primitives over leaf fixes. Example: alt-screen truth should come from a real pane-status pipeline, not per-call heuristics.
5. Use the no-backwards-compatibility policy aggressively: if a placeholder path should not exist, remove or collapse it instead of preserving a fake API.
6. Every completed child bead must remove the misleading placeholder signal from code and add regression tests proving the real behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"- Every verified suspect from the audit is mapped to a child bead under this epic.
- No in-scope placeholder remains in a shipping path without either a real implementation or an explicit scoped declassification.
- Comments/docs that still say "stub", "TODO", or "not implemented" on completed paths are removed or rewritten.
- Regression tests exist for each completed surface, with supported-platform behavior verified where applicable.
- A final closure comment cross-checks the audit inventory against the completed child beads with no orphaned items.
- Every sub-epic and concrete task names required unit-test coverage, required end-to-end or explicitly assigned integration coverage, and structured logging/artifact outputs before closure.
- Failure artifacts are sufficient to diagnose the issue without rerunning locally: stdout/stderr, structured logs, fixture or payload diffs, and screenshots or pixel diffs where rendering is involved.","notes":"Out-of-scope unless reclassified by evidence: test-only fake panes, intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`, unsupported-platform `Option::None` style shims, and vendored/upstream dependency TODOs.

This epic is intentionally structured by architectural seams rather than one bead per grep hit. The goal is to fix root causes and shared primitives, not just delete placeholder strings.

## Verification Doctrine
- Follow the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Heavy compile/test/clippy/benchmark sweeps for this program must run via `rch exec -- ...`.
- Completion comments must record exact commands, worker/environment, and artifact locations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:27:33.773255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.501104Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:56:02.500840Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","rendering","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"comments":[{"id":2740,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"This epic is the execution wrapper for the 2026-04-02 placeholder/stub audit. The child tracks are ordered to minimize wasted work:
- machine-control and client behavior first,
- search/connector trust next,
- core runtime and FTUI after shared state surfaces are real,
- rendering/backend parity after higher-risk control paths,
- mux/SSH operational gaps in parallel where isolated.

Future-self rule: if a new suspect appears while working these beads, either attach it to the appropriate child bead or create a new child under this epic immediately. Do not let the inventory drift back into ad hoc comments.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":3545,"issue_id":"ft-akx00","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure ledger: All 8 children closed. ft-akx00.1 (closure ledger), ft-akx00.2 (machine-control/SDK), ft-akx00.3 (operator-support/persistence), ft-akx00.4 (connector/search), ft-akx00.5 (runtime/scrollback/recording/FTUI), ft-akx00.6 (rendering/window backends), ft-akx00.7 (tmux/mux-domain/SSH), ft-akx00.8 (shared verification contract). Full placeholder-remediation program complete.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:55:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.1","title":"Maintain closure ledger for placeholder-remediation program","description":"# Purpose
Maintain the closure ledger for this remediation program so the audit remains complete and falsifiable.

This bead owns the suspect matrix, scope decisions, and final proof that every audited placeholder ended in a deliberate outcome. It exists to prevent the classic failure mode where implementation starts but the inventory stops being tracked.

# Deliverables
- A suspect-by-suspect ledger mapping each audited item to a child bead and final disposition.
- Explicit documentation for any item that gets reclassified as intentional or out-of-scope.
- Final cross-check before the parent epic closes.","design":"Treat the original audit as an immutable input set. For each suspect, record:
- exact file/function/branch,
- why it was considered placeholder behavior,
- which remediation bead owns it,
- what proof is required to declare it closed.

If a task discovers that the optimal fix is deletion or API collapse, record that explicitly here so future readers know it was a principled simplification rather than an omission.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ledger names every verified suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit.
- Each suspect points to exactly one owning child bead or an explicit documented reclassification.
- The final comment on this bead reports any deltas between the initial inventory and the executed work.
- The ledger records not only ownership but also the verification surface for each audited placeholder: unit tests, E2E/integration coverage, and artifact expectations.","notes":"Reopened: stalled 13-22 days with no recent activity. Prior claimant likely inactive.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageHorse","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.899175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:16.898914Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55.213472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2741,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Initial ledger categories:
- Control plane/client: NTM stub response, client workspace/unilateral handling, client spawn/alt-screen, robot SDK transport.
- Operator support: chrono stub, env-bootstrap/ringlog stubs, scheduled backup compression.
- Search/connectors: transparency verification, certification probe, reranker, model2vec, fastembed sentinel behavior.
- Core runtime/UX: NullPaneScrollbackAccess, recording encodings, FTUI placeholder views, stale PaneCapabilities stub comment.
- Rendering/backends: Wayland seat+keyboard, WGL/macOS/EGL resize, bitmap/WebGPU readback, terminfo raw/crop images, background sizing, custom glyph placeholders.
- Mux/SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle, detach semantics, SFTP metadata, SSH Match Exec.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:55Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Inventory-to-bead ledger for the 2026-04-02 placeholder audit:

Control plane / client / SDK
- `build_ntm_stub_response` fake `"status": "stub"` payload -> `ft-akx00.2.1`
- `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO + unilateral-message FIXME -> `ft-akx00.2.2`
- `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + `ClientPane::is_alt_screen_active()` hardcoded false -> `ft-akx00.2.3`
- generated Rust SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` -> `ft-akx00.2.4`

Operator support / persistence utilities
- `chrono_stub_now()` -> `ft-akx00.3.1`
- `env-bootstrap` / `ringlog` stub + no-op logger -> `ft-akx00.3.2`
- scheduled backup compression placeholder -> `ft-akx00.3.3`

Connector / search
- connector transparency verification stub -> `ft-akx00.4.1`
- certification integration probe stub -> `ft-akx00.4.2`
- `CrossEncoderReranker` identity/no-op stub -> `ft-akx00.4.3`
- `Model2VecEmbedder` stub + FastEmbed sentinel constructor -> `ft-akx00.4.4`

Core runtime / FTUI / policy
- `NullPaneScrollbackAccess` placeholder -> `ft-akx00.5.1`
- `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `Repeat` placeholders -> `ft-akx00.5.2`
- FTUI placeholder view bodies -> `ft-akx00.5.3`
- stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation in `policy.rs` -> `ft-akx00.5.4`

Rendering / windowing
- Wayland seat + keyboard TODO/unimplemented paths -> `ft-akx00.6.1`
- WGL/macOS/EGL resize + framebuffer gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.2`
- bitmap/WebGPU texture readback gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.3`
- terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps -> `ft-akx00.6.4`
- GUI background sizing + custom glyph placeholder rendering -> `ft-akx00.6.5`

Mux / SSH
- tmux PTY `try_wait()` + kill placeholders -> `ft-akx00.7.1`
- detach unimplemented across domains -> `ft-akx00.7.2`
- SFTP metadata mutation placeholder -> `ft-akx00.7.3`
- SSH `Match Exec` omission -> `ft-akx00.7.4`

Deliberate exclusions from this epic unless later reclassified by evidence:
- test-only fake panes and other `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- intentional null-object helpers like `NoopCaptureSink`
- unsupported-platform no-op shims used only to represent platform absence
- vendored/upstream TODOs outside first-party source","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:33:17Z"},{"id":2798,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Verification-matrix reminder:
- Shared contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`
- Every concrete child under `ft-akx00` now requires both comprehensive unit tests and a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario with structured logs and artifacts.
- Completion evidence for future implementation beads should cite the exact proposed script name (or renamed final equivalent), exact commands, artifact root, and whether heavy validation ran via `rch`.","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:52Z"},{"id":2813,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"SageHorse","text":"Ledger update for `ft-akx00.5.2` (closed 2026-04-03):

Suspect:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`: dormant `DeltaEncoding::Diff` / `DeltaEncoding::Repeat` placeholder branches plus `DiffOp` placeholder type.

Disposition:
- Closed by API collapse, not by implementing fake delta machinery. Recording WAR v1 now exposes only `DeltaEncoding::Full(Vec<u8>)`, and the spec was updated to say so explicitly. This is a principled narrowing of scope, not an omission.

Proof captured:
- Code/docs updated: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs`, `docs/recording-format-spec.md`.
- Regression: serde test now rejects the removed legacy placeholder variants.
- Targeted verification passed: `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_recording` (47 tests).
- Local formatting proof: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_recording.rs crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_reranker.rs`.

Verification notes / non-bead blockers:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` remains blocked by remote worker env drift (`x11-xcb.pc` missing on worker `vmi1149989`).
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` remains blocked by existing failures in `frankenterm/config/derive` and `frankenterm/dynamic/derive`.
- Fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets` got past earlier test exhaustiveness drift and now fails only on unrelated bench debt in `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/input_latency_bench.rs` and `crates/frankenterm-core/benches/spike_comparison.rs`.

Cross-reference:
- Owning bead: `ft-akx00.5.2`
- Verification contract bead: `ft-akx00.8`

Follow-up for this ledger bead: accumulate the same disposition/proof block for each remaining child bead so the parent epic can be closed against a falsifiable matrix rather than scattered thread history.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:25:13Z"},{"id":3541,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"# ft-akx00 Closure Ledger — Final Cross-Check (FoggyRobin, 2026-04-19)

## Inventory Status: 30/32 beads closed, 1 in_progress, 1 open (blocked)

| ft-akx00.2.3 | `ClientDomain::spawn_pane` unimplemented + alt-screen hardcoded false | Real remote protocol via `ListPanesResponse` + delta tracking | PlumDesert | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.2.4 | SDK transport `unimplemented!("transport not wired")` | Wired `RustSdkTransport` to IPC/RPC backend | BeigeBluff | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.2 | Epic rollup | All 4 children closed | jemanuel | CLOSED |

| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|

| ft-akx00.3.2 | env-bootstrap/ringlog stub logger | Graduated to real `env_logger::try_init()` + OnceLock idempotency | jemanuel | CLOSED |

### Track 6: Rendering, Window Backends [4/5 closed, 1 in_progress]
| Bead | Suspect | Disposition | Agent | Status |
|------|---------|------------|-------|--------|
| ft-akx00.6.1 | Wayland seat/keyboard `todo!()` paths | Real seat teardown + keyboard leave handling | SilentSpire/BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.2 | WGL/macOS/EGL resize/framebuffer gaps | Confirmed implemented; backing-size normalization added | IndigoFox/BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.3 | Bitmap/WebGPU texture readback `unimplemented!()` | Implementation in progress | jemanuel | IN_PROGRESS |
| ft-akx00.6.4 | Terminfo raw/cropped image rendering gaps | Implemented iTerm2 image rendering for raw/cropped/animated RGBA | IndigoFox | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6.5 | GUI background-sizing + custom glyph placeholders | Real `Contain`/`Cover` + scalable diagonal hatches | BrightCrane | CLOSED |
| ft-akx00.6 | Epic rollup | Blocked by ft-akx00.6.3 | — | OPEN |

## Deliberate Exclusions (unchanged from initial audit)
- Test-only fake panes and `#[cfg(test)]` doubles
- Intentional null-object helpers (e.g., `NoopCaptureSink`)
- Unsupported-platform no-op shims representing platform absence

## Gap Analysis
**Only remaining gap**: ft-akx00.6.3 (texture readback across bitmap/WebGPU) — currently in_progress, assigned to jemanuel. Once this closes, ft-akx00.6 epic and then the parent ft-akx00 epic can close.

**No orphaned items**: Every suspect from the 2026-04-02 audit maps to exactly one child bead. No suspects were lost or reclassified without documentation.

**Infrastructure blockers noted but not code-level**: Several rch workers lack platform toolchains (wayland-client.pc, x11.pc, Windows/macOS cross-CC). These limit CI verification breadth but do not indicate missing code.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:25:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-phbu5","title":"[HIGH] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology still cannot verify pane-tree rollback","description":"Commit 30172171 fixes the vacuous baseline roundtrip, but the test still reduces "topology" to a pane-id set derived from LifecycleRegistry records rather than the restored topology payload.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs:392-423` now compares only `server.registry().snapshot()` pane IDs/count against the baseline and then re-parses `initial_topo_json` as a secondary serde check.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs:38-89` defines topology as windows, tabs, `active_tab_index`, and recursive `pane_tree`, not just pane membership.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/durable_state.rs:159-167,288-314` shows HeadlessMuxServer checkpoints/rollback persist and rebuild only `LifecycleRegistry::snapshot()` entities; there is no topology JSON in the checkpoint path for the test to compare after rollback.

Why this matters:
- A rollback that preserves the same pane IDs but restores the wrong tab grouping, active tab, or split tree would still satisfy the current assertions.
- The test name and comments still claim topology restoration coverage, so this is false confidence on the checkpoint/rollback surface.

Expected fix:
- Either persist/restore topology payloads in the checkpoint path and compare a post-rollback `TopologySnapshot` against the baseline,
- or explicitly narrow/rename this test to registry-membership rollback and add a separate topology persistence/restore integration test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:58:05.819749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.021289Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.020891Z","close_reason":"Strengthened full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology with structural pane-tree rollback invariant","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-qf3vk","title":"[HIGH] ft-ybtyg incomplete: session_checkpoints.state_hash is written but never verified on load","description":"Follow-up from ft-ybtyg (closed in 598a7c13).

## Gap

The ft-ybtyg fix replaced the placeholder `'restore'` literal with a real SipHash via `compute_restore_state_hash` and documented the intent: 'a later integrity check can recompute and compare'. But grep across session_restore.rs + snapshot_engine.rs + storage.rs finds ZERO callers that read the stored state_hash, recompute from the current inputs, and assert equality. The hash is written on INSERT, never verified on SELECT/load.

Result: the on-disk state_hash column now contains useful-looking SipHash values but is still effectively unverified — an attacker who tampers with the session_checkpoints row (flipping hash + any other field) is not detected, because no read path compares the stored hash against the recomputed hash.

## Reproducer
```
grep -rn 'compute_restore_state_hash\|compute_state_hash' crates/frankenterm-core/src/ | grep -iE 'verify|check|compare|assert_eq'
```
Expected matches: any call site that does `recomputed == stored`.
Actual: (empty).

## Fix direction
1. In the session-load path (session_restore.rs load fn + snapshot_engine.rs open/restore), after reading a session_checkpoints row, call compute_restore_state_hash on the current inputs and compare with the stored state_hash column.
2. On mismatch, return a typed Error (new StateHashMismatch variant) and refuse to restore from a tampered checkpoint.
3. Add regression test with a deliberately-tampered row that must produce StateHashMismatch and NOT a silent restore.

## Severity
MEDIUM → HIGH. The fix closed the docs-drift half but left the verification half open. A partial integrity check is worse than no check because it signals a stronger trust story than actually exists.

## Priority
P1 — this closes a real hole that ft-ybtyg was supposed to close.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:03:26.056799Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:28:42.029163Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:28:42.028931Z","close_reason":"fixed in ca3d3fe2 — state_hash verification wired into load_checkpoint_by_id for startup rows; StateHashMismatch error + 5 regression tests (tampered hash, tampered pane_id_map, legacy 'restore' literal, null metadata, non-startup skip). Periodic/capture checkpoints remain a documented follow-up since their hash is over PaneInfo not reconstructable from session_checkpoints alone.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-qzunb","title":"[HIGH] mux layout_arrangement + swap_layout JSON proptests fail on f64 ratio precision drift","description":"layout_arrangement_json_roundtrip and swap_layout_json_roundtrip in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs currently fail at HEAD. arb_layout_arrangement generates LayoutArrangement::Split { ratio: 0.0f64..=1.0f64, ... } with recursive depth. When that ratio is serialized via serde_json and parsed back, some unrounded f64 values lose precision in the last decimal place (e.g. 0.10814827875804041 → 0.1081482787580404 and 0.9177432348398312 → 0.9177432348398313), so the roundtrip prop_assert_eq!(back, value) fails.

This is a blocker: the mux crate's cargo test currently shows 2/9 failing.

Fix direction: restrict the ratio generator to values that are exactly representable in IEEE 754 f64 with decimal notation (e.g. rounded to 1/2^16 fractions, or integer tenths/hundredths). Preferred: generate as integer 0..=10_000 and divide by 10_000.0 so every ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives JSON's decimal representation. Keep the recursive depth/size parameters as-is.

Repro:
  CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \
    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane6-target \
    cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --test proptest_tab_wire_serde

Observed at HEAD (04dc8d76).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:59:44.814922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542629Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:01:15.542244Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 10309c8e: constrain arb_layout_arrangement's ratio to (0..=10_000).map(|n| n as f64 / 10_000) so every generated ratio is of the form n/10_000 and survives a serde_json decimal roundtrip bit-for-bit. All 10 tests in frankenterm/mux/tests/proptest_tab_wire_serde.rs now pass (was 7 pass / 2 fail). Granularity 0.0001 is more than adequate for split layout wire data.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","mux","proptest","regression","serde"]}
 {"id":"ft-r4l82","title":"Mock-code-finder sweep of mux/term/codec: 0 production stubs, ft-yv02l #6-7 re-triage","description":"Multi-method mock-code-finder sweep of frankenterm/mux (25 files), frankenterm/term (26 files), frankenterm/codec (5 files) on 2026-04-20.

## Methods
- Keyword grep: TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/STUB/PLACEHOLDER/MOCK/DUMMY/FAKE/TEMP
- unimplemented!() / todo!() scan
- Short-function/fallback-return scan: -> bool { true/false }, -> Option<_> { None }, empty bodies
- Hedge-phrase grep: not yet, for now, best effort, no-op

## Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Verdict |
|---|----------|----------|---------|
| 1-29 | mux/src/pane.rs:603-775 + mux/src/tab.rs:4027-5100+ | 29x unimplemented!() in FakePane test doubles | False positive - inside #[cfg(test)] modules (pane.rs:595, tab.rs:4004). Legitimate Rust pattern: test doubles only impl trait methods the tests exercise. |
| 30 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:141 | // TODO: selective_erase when supported (struct-field comment) | Documentation of known terminal-emulator limitation. Not a stub - the struct works without this field. |
| 31 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:1251 | // TODO: see https://vt100.net/docs/vt510-rm/DECSTR.html in Device::SoftReset | Reference comment. The SoftReset handler is fully implemented below - comment is just a spec pointer. |
| 32-33 | term/src/test/c0.rs:13,36 | // TODO: when we can set the left margin, we should test that here | Test-coverage gap. Tests still run; two cases arent covered because the test framework doesnt expose left-margin setup. |
| 34 | term/src/test/mod.rs:9 | // mod selection; FIXME: port to render layer | Historical module disable. The selection test module was moved to the render layer; this comment is a bread-crumb. |
| 35 | mux/src/ssh.rs:957 | fn process_id(&self) -> Option<u32> { None } | Correct by design - WrappedSshChild has no local PID because the child process runs on the remote SSH host. None is the honest answer. |
| 36 | mux/src/ssh.rs:962 | fn as_raw_handle(&self) -> Option<RawHandle> { None } (Windows) | Same rationale as #35. |

## Corrects prior record

The closed bead ft-yv02l (2026-04-20 'Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep') listed the mux tab.rs and pane.rs unimplemented!() clusters under 'Cat 2' and deferred them as 'out-of-scope, pane 1 (mux) territory' requiring domain-owner review. That classification was incorrect - these are test scaffolding inside #[cfg(test)] modules. There is no pane-1-owner action required.

## Outcome

- 0 production stubs
- 0 actionable TODOs (all 5 are documentation / test-coverage / historical disables)
- 0 commits required
- Memory note recorded for future sweeps

## Why this bead exists

Audit trail so future /mock-code-finder passes over the frankenterm/mux|term|codec cluster can skip the scan (its mature WezTerm-heritage code) and correct the residual noise from ft-yv02l #6-7.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:33:07.647071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416510Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416201Z","close_reason":"Verified ft-yv02l #6-7 are #[cfg(test)] FakePane scaffolding only; no production stub to fix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-riz42","title":"Migrate wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl from direct smol to asupersync","description":"Both crates have direct smol::Async<T>, smol::channel, smol::future::or, and async_io::IoSafe usage that needs asupersync equivalents. Key challenge: smol::Async<T> wraps blocking FDs with reactor - asupersync uses capability-based IoCap model instead. Needs design work for the I/O wrapping layer. Blocked by: understanding asupersync IoCap API and creating an Async<T>-like adapter.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmCreek","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:02:11.906232Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.015173Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:01.014992Z","close_reason":"Completed direct Smol removal in wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl; remaining debt tracked in ft-xbnl0.2.1 verification finish-line work","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2733,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"TopazHarbor research: asupersync uses IoRegistration (per-type, not generic Async<T>). TcpStream/UnixStream each embed Option<IoRegistration> directly. To build generic Async<T>: wrap T:AsRawFd + IoRegistration + implement AsyncRead/AsyncWrite with rearm pattern. Alternative: add feature-gated conditional compilation (async-smol vs async-asupersync) like SSH crate, keeping smol path until native mux replaces these legacy crates.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:22:30Z"},{"id":2827,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Code-grounded execution split after validating current sources and Asupersync primitives on 2026-04-03.

What the code says now
- `frankenterm/client` and `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl` are the direct Smol entrypoints.
- Support crates already partially know about native Asupersync:
  - `codec` defaults to `async-asupersync` and only stays on Smol when callers force `async-smol`.
  - `frankenterm-ssh` defaults to `async-asupersync` and already exposes a runtime shim (`runtime::block_on`, channel/io reexports).
  - `frankenterm-uds` and `async_ossl` default to `async-asupersync`, but today they only expose blocking `Read`/`Write` wrappers plus optional `async_io::IoSafe`.
- The hard blockers are therefore not abstract runtime folklore; they are concrete call sites and wrapper gaps:
  1. local Unix / mux-server dispatch path
  2. SSH proxy / pipe path
  3. TLS/OpenSSL path

Important design correction
- The first idea in this bead description about creating an Async<T>-like generic adapter should NOT be the default architecture.
- Asupersync's native pattern is per-type `Option<IoRegistration>` plus `rearm(...)` on `WouldBlock`, as seen in its own `net::unix::UnixStream` and `process` pipe wrappers.
- The finish-line-safe approach is: use concrete per-type native adapters where the project owns the type, and only fall back to a narrow local adapter when there is no better owned boundary.

Recommended implementation slicing
1. Local Unix + mux-server dispatch first.
   files: `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, relevant `Cargo.toml` files.
   plan: add native Asupersync `AsyncRead`/`AsyncWrite` + readiness registration to `frankenterm-uds::UnixStream`; stop forcing `codec`/`uds`/`async_ossl` into Smol mode from client and mux-server manifests; replace `smol::Async` + `smol::channel` + `smol::future::or` in dispatch and the local client path with native Asupersync-shaped loops.

2. SSH proxy / pipe path second.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, possibly `frankenterm/ssh/*` only if needed.
   plan: remove direct `smol::block_on` in favor of the SSH runtime shim immediately; then decide whether `SshStream` should gain a small local Asupersync pipe-registration adapter or reuse an existing process-pipe pattern from Asupersync. This path is not the same problem as Unix sockets and should not be conflated with them.

3. TLS/OpenSSL path third, coordinated with finish-line networking work.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   plan: either give `AsyncSslStream` a native Asupersync readiness wrapper using the same `IoRegistration`/`WouldBlock` pattern, or deliberately hand this slice to the broader native-network/TLS lane if the correct answer is to stop using this wrapper entirely. Do not hide TLS behind another executor-neutral shim.

Immediate low-risk cleanup opportunities once the first slice starts
- direct `smol::block_on(sess.exec(...))` in `client.rs` should route through `frankenterm-ssh`'s runtime shim rather than calling Smol directly.
- `smol::Timer::after(...)` in `client.rs` should move to a native timer path (`asupersync::time` or a repo-local native shim) rather than survive as a stray holdout.

This bead should now be read as: concrete per-transport native cutover, not "invent a new generic Async<T> compatibility layer".","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:04:26Z"},{"id":2831,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Progress update 2026-04-04.

Completed in-code cleanup before the larger stream-wrapper migration:
- `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`
  - `client_thread(...)` now uses `promise::spawn::block_on(...)` instead of importing Smol `block_on`.
  - SSH exec call sites now use `wezterm_ssh::runtime::block_on(...)` instead of `smol::block_on(...)`.
  - version-compat timeout now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.
- `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`
  - title propagation debounce now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.

This does NOT close the bead. Remaining direct Smol/async-io debt in `client.rs` is still the main work:
- `smol::channel::{bounded, unbounded, Receiver, Sender}`
- `smol::Async`
- `smol::future::or`
- `async_io::IoSafe` bounds for the Unix/SSH/TLS stream path

Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail health is green, but stateful reads/writes are currently failing with `DATABASE_ERROR` / `no such table: projects`, so file reservation and inbox checks are temporarily unavailable from this session.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:08:29Z"},{"id":2834,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Implemented the first native Asupersync transport slice in frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs: UnixStream now carries feature-gated async-asupersync AsyncRead/AsyncWrite(+vectored) impls backed by lazy nonblocking conversion plus IoRegistration re-arm, without removing the existing blocking Read/Write surface. Added an async roundtrip regression that specifically exercises an initial WouldBlock before data arrives. Remote cargo check caught a broad test ambiguity from importing AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt at module scope; that is fixed by scoping the imports to the new async test, and a fresh rch check is now in flight. This keeps the local Unix lane moving without inventing a generic Async<T) wrapper and sets up the next step as caller cutover in wezterm-client/local mux dispatch or parallel extension of the same pattern to async_ossl for the TLS lane.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:24:55Z"},{"id":2839,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-04 progress update.

Verified remotely via rch:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p async_ossl --all-targets` passed after fixing the dual-trait vectored write ambiguity in `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client --all-targets` passed after moving `wezterm-client` Unix/TLS transport ownership onto the native wrappers: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs` now boxes raw `UnixStream` and `AsyncSslStream` directly, and `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` now requests `wezterm-uds`/`async_ossl` with `async-asupersync` features instead of `async-io`. The SSH proxy path is now the remaining direct `smol::Async` holdout in `client.rs`.

`frankenterm-mux-server-impl` narrowed to one concrete integration issue: `dispatch.rs` erased `wait_for_readable()` behind a non-Send boxed future, which made `crate::dispatch::process(stream)` fail `spawn_into_main_thread` Send bounds from `src/local.rs`. I tightened the dispatch trait to require a Send boxed readiness future and kicked off a fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets`; result still pending as of this comment.

Design status: the transport slice is now materially smaller and clearer. Local Unix and TLS are on the owned Asupersync-backed stream types; the next native cut after this rerun is the SSH split-FD stream and then the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:58:56Z"},{"id":2840,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"jemanuel","text":"Follow-up verification: the fresh rerun `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets` passed after tightening `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs` so `DispatchStream::wait_for_readable()` returns a Send boxed future. That clears the local Unix/TLS transport slice across `async_ossl`, `wezterm-client`, and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl`; the remaining direct Smol surface in this bead is now concentrated in the SSH proxy stream plus the smol channel/or loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:59:45Z"},{"id":2923,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Taking the next concrete slice of ft-riz42: switching wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl onto codec async-asupersync, replacing their remaining smol channel/or coordination loops, and removing the SSH smol::Async<SshStream> wrapper in favor of a native split-FD readiness wrapper. I’m reading the filedescriptor/asupersync APIs now before patching.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:09:27Z"},{"id":2925,"issue_id":"ft-riz42","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Implemented the remaining direct Smol cut in the current ft-riz42 slice. Changes: frankenterm/client and crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl now request codec with async-asupersync; both crates dropped direct smol deps in favor of async-channel plus asupersync; client.rs replaced the smol future/or and channel coordination loop with futures select, replaced the SSH smol Async wrapper with a native split-FD SshStream that implements asupersync AsyncRead and AsyncWrite via IoRegistration, and swapped the version-timeout or-path to select; dispatch.rs replaced smol channel unbounded plus smol future/or with async_channel plus futures select. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets passed after the borrow and lifetime fixes; broad rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets is running now.
